subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Habitat Tiles
- **85 habitat tiles** — Hexagonal tiles showing 1-2 terrain types.
- **5 terrain types** — Mountains, Forests, Prairies, Wetlands, Rivers.
- **Wildlife icons** — Each tile shows 1-3 wildlife icons indicating which animals can be placed there.

#### Wildlife Tokens
- **100 wildlife tokens** — Wooden tokens in 5 types.
- **5 wildlife types** — Bear, Elk, Salmon, Hawk, Fox.
- **20 tokens each** — 20 per wildlife type.

#### Scoring Cards
- **Wildlife scoring cards** — 3 cards per wildlife type (A, B, C variants).
- **5 selected per game** — One scoring card per wildlife type; choose all A, all B, all C, or mix.

#### Other Components
- **Starter habitat tiles** — 5 larger 3-hex starter tiles (1 per player).
- **Nature tokens** — 25 pine cone tokens; players start with 1 each.
- **Cloth bag** — For drawing wildlife tokens.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Select scoring cards** — Choose 1 wildlife scoring card per type (e.g., all A-sides for beginners).
2. **Display scoring cards** — Place 5 scoring cards face up for reference.
3. **Shuffle habitat tiles** — Shuffle all 85 habitat tiles face down.
4. **Form tile stacks** — Create face-down stacks.
5. **Fill wildlife bag** — Place all 100 wildlife tokens in the bag.
6. **Create shared display** — Reveal 4 habitat tiles in a row; draw 4 wildlife tokens and pair each with a tile.
7. **Nature token supply** — Place remaining nature tokens in a shared pool.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Receives
- **1 starter tile** — A 3-hex starter habitat tile placed in front of you.
- **1 nature token** — Take 1 nature token from the supply.

#### Starter Tiles
- **3 hexes** — Each starter tile contains 3 connected hexes with various terrain types.
- **Wildlife icons** — Each hex shows which wildlife can be placed there.
- **Starting landscape** — Your starter tile is the foundation you build around.

--- Solo Mode ---
- **Solo scoring targets** — Score thresholds to beat for bronze, silver, gold achievements.

--- 2-4 Players ---
- **Standard play** — Each player builds their own landscape independently.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Shared display ready** — 4 tile-token pairs visible.
2. **Youngest player starts** — Or choose randomly.
3. **Clockwise turns** — Play proceeds clockwise.
4. **First turn** — Draft 1 tile-token pair from the display.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### On Your Turn
1. **Draft a pair** — Select 1 habitat tile and its paired wildlife token from the display of 4.
2. **Place habitat tile** — Add the tile to your landscape, adjacent to any existing tile.
3. **Place wildlife token** — Place the wildlife token on any tile in your landscape that shows that wildlife's icon and does not already have a wildlife token.
4. **Optionally discard token** — If you cannot or do not want to place the wildlife token, return it to the bag.
5. **Refill display** — Draw a new habitat tile and wildlife token to refill the display back to 4 pairs.

#### Nature Token Use
- **Spend a nature token** — Instead of taking a paired tile-token, spend a nature token to take any tile and any token independently (unpaired).
- **Overpopulation reset** — If 3 or more of the same wildlife token appear in the display, clear all wildlife tokens, draw new ones, and each player gains 1 nature token.
- **Earn nature tokens** — Gained from overpopulation resets.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Drafting Options
- **Standard draft** — Take 1 tile and its paired token (free).
- **Nature token draft** — Spend 1 nature token to take any tile and any token from the display independently.

#### Habitat Tile Placement
- **Adjacency required** — New tile must share at least 1 edge with an existing tile.
- **No terrain matching required** — Tiles can be placed regardless of adjacent terrain types.
- **Contiguous groups** — Matching adjacent terrains form contiguous habitat groups for bonus scoring.

#### Wildlife Token Placement
- **Matching icon** — Token must be placed on a tile showing that wildlife type's icon.
- **No existing token** — Tile must not already have a wildlife token.
- **Optional** — You may choose not to place the token (return to bag).

#### Wildlife Scoring Patterns (vary by card)
- **Bear** — Typically scores for pairs or groups of specific sizes.
- **Elk** — Typically scores for straight lines of adjacent elk tokens.
- **Salmon** — Typically scores for runs (chains) of salmon that do not loop.
- **Hawk** — Typically scores for hawks not adjacent to other hawks.
- **Fox** — Typically scores based on variety of other wildlife adjacent to each fox.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Habitat Corridors
- **Contiguous terrain groups** — Connected hexes of the same terrain type form a corridor.
- **Largest group bonus** — At game end, the player with the largest contiguous group of each terrain type scores bonus points.
- **Ties** — All tied players receive the bonus.
- **5 terrain types** — Each terrain type has its own largest-group comparison.

#### Nature Tokens
- **Flexibility tool** — Spend to break the tile-token pairing and draft independently.
- **Unused tokens** — Each unspent nature token at game end is worth 1 VP.
- **Overpopulation trigger** — 3+ identical wildlife tokens in the display triggers a reset and grants tokens to all players.

#### Scoring Card Variants
- **A cards** — Simpler scoring patterns (recommended for first game).
- **B cards** — Moderate complexity scoring patterns.
- **C cards** — Advanced scoring patterns with higher point ceilings.
- **Mix and match** — Choose different letter variants per wildlife type for variety.

#### Keystone Tiles
- **Single wildlife icon** — Tiles showing only 1 wildlife type are more restrictive but can be strategically placed.
- **Multiple icons** — Tiles showing 2-3 wildlife types give more flexibility but attract competition.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Tiles run out** — The game ends when the habitat tile supply is exhausted (20 turns per player in a standard game).
- **Final scoring** — Proceed to final scoring after all players complete their last turn.

#### Scoring Categories
- **Bear scoring** — Score per the bear scoring card's pattern.
- **Elk scoring** — Score per the elk scoring card's pattern.
- **Salmon scoring** — Score per the salmon scoring card's pattern.
- **Hawk scoring** — Score per the hawk scoring card's pattern.
- **Fox scoring** — Score per the fox scoring card's pattern.
- **Habitat bonus** — For each of the 5 terrain types, the player(s) with the largest contiguous group scores bonus VP.
- **Nature tokens** — Each unspent nature token = 1 VP.

#### Winner
- **Most total VP wins** — Sum all wildlife scoring + habitat bonuses + nature tokens.
- **Tiebreaker** — Player with the most nature tokens remaining wins; if still tied, shared victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Read all 5 scoring cards** — Understand each wildlife's scoring pattern before drafting.
- **Prioritize high-value wildlife** — Some scoring cards reward much more per token than others; focus there.
- **Build terrain corridors** — Start connecting same-terrain hexes early for habitat bonuses.
- **Keep nature tokens** — They are worth 1 VP each and provide flexibility; do not spend frivolously.
- **Draft keystone tiles early** — Single-icon tiles are restrictive but guarantee placement for specific wildlife.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring habitat bonuses** — Terrain corridor majorities add significant points; do not treat them as an afterthought.
- **Placing wildlife randomly** — Each token placement should serve the scoring card's pattern; random placement wastes potential.
- **Hoarding nature tokens** — They are worth 1 VP each, but a well-timed spend can net much more than 1 VP.
- **Tunnel vision on one wildlife** — Neglecting other wildlife types leaves easy points on the table.
- **Forgetting to check all pairs** — Sometimes a less-obvious tile-token pair is better when you consider both the tile and token value.
- **Over-expanding terrain** — Building one massive terrain group beyond what is needed for the bonus is wasteful.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Tile vs token priority** — Decide whether the habitat tile or the wildlife token is the main draw from each pair.
- **Nature token timing** — When to spend a nature token for an unpaired draft vs saving for end-game VP.
- **Habitat group investment** — Whether to keep growing a terrain corridor for the bonus or diversify.
- **Wildlife clustering** — Whether to cluster wildlife for pattern scoring or spread them for flexibility.
- **React to display** — Adapt your strategy based on what tiles and tokens appear in the shared display.
